title: Why Are Some Emotions Harder for LLMs? Uncovering the Causal Mechanisms of Emotion Inference via Sparse Autoencoders
abstract: arXiv:2604.25866v2 Announce Type: replace  Abstract: Large language models (LLMs) are increasingly used in emotionally sensitive human-AI applications, where reliable emotion detection is essential. However, their emotion recognition abilities remain uneven: models often perform well on some emotions while consistently struggling with others. Although recent work has explored emotion mechanisms in LLMs, little is known about why models are weaker on some emotions than others from a mechanistic interpretability perspective. In this work, we investigate emotion-specific biases through the causal mechanisms of emotion inference using sparse autoencoders (SAEs). We systematically identify causal sparse emotion features that drive emotion inference and analyze their sparse causal organization within and across emotions. We show that some emotions, such as surprise and fear, rely on highly concentrated feature sets, whereas disgust exhibits a more distributed sparse causal organization: its

translated_title: 为什么某些情绪对大语言模型更难？通过稀疏自编码器揭示情绪推断的因果机制
translated_abstract: arXiv:2604.25866v2 公告类型：替换 摘要：大语言模型（LLMs）越来越多地用于情感敏感的人机交互应用中，在这些应用中，可靠的情绪检测至关重要。然而，它们的情绪识别能力仍然不均衡：模型通常在某些情绪上表现良好，而在其他情绪上却持续表现不佳。尽管最近的研究探索了LLMs中的情绪机制，但从机械可解释性的角度，我们对于为什么模型在某些情绪上比其他情绪更弱的原因知之甚少。在这项工作中，我们通过使用稀疏自编码器（SAEs）研究情绪推断的因果机制，来探究情绪特定偏差。我们系统地识别了驱动情绪推断的因果稀疏情绪特征，并分析了它们在情绪内部和跨情绪之间的稀疏因果组织。我们发现，某些情绪（如惊讶和恐惧）依赖于高度集中的特征集，而厌恶则表现出更分散的稀疏因果组织：其
tldr: 本研究通过稀疏自编码器揭示了不同情绪在LLM中推断机制的因果差异，发现惊讶和恐惧依赖集中特征集，而厌恶则依赖更分散的因果组织，这解释了模型对不同情绪表现不均的原因。
en_tdlr: This study uncovers causal differences in emotion inference mechanisms in LLMs via sparse autoencoders, revealing that surprise and fear rely on concentrated feature sets while disgust depends on more distributed causal organization, explaining the uneven performance of models across emotions.
